key_learnings: Glycemic index (GI) and glycemic load (GL) of foods are important but must be contextualized within overall meal composition — fat, fiber, and protein all lower postprandial glucose response. Low-carbohydrate diets are the most effective dietary intervention for reducing HbA1c in type-2 diabetes. Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data reveals highly individual glycemic responses to the same foods.
research_findings: Low-carb diets reduce HbA1c by 0.5–1.5% in type-2 diabetic patients across multiple RCTs. The PREDIMED trial demonstrated Mediterranean diet reduces type-2 diabetes incidence by 52% vs. low-fat control. Vinegar and cinnamon consumption modestly improve post-meal insulin sensitivity via different mechanisms.
current_focus_areas: Integrating CGM-derived personal glycemic response data into dietary recommendations; developing carbohydrate-sequencing protocols (eating fiber/protein before carbs) to blunt glucose spikes; building risk stratification for prediabetes dietary intervention.
